div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union (Kirk) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
3123. Press prominently reports arrival Mao Tze-tung Moscow yesterday accompanied by Professor Chen Po-ta and others unnamed. Was received same day at Kremlin by Stalin, Molotov,V. M. Molotov, Senior Deputy Chairman of the Council of Ministers of the Soviet Union. Malenkov,G. M. Malenkov, Deputy Chairman of the Council of Ministers of the Soviet Union. BulganinMarshal N. A. Bulganin, Deputy Chairman of the Council of Ministers of the Soviet Union. and Vishinsky.A. Ya. Vyshinsky, Soviet Minister for Foreign Affairs. At railroad station to meet him were following: Molotov, Bulganin, Menshikov,Mikhail Alexeyevich Menshikov, Soviet Minister of Foreign Trade. Gromyko,A. A. Gromyko, Soviet First Deputy Minister for Foreign Affairs. Moscow Commandant
Sinilov, Chief First FE Section, MFA, Kuryukov, Acting Chief Protocol Section, MFA, B.U. Shuyev, Chinese Ambassador and staff, Czech, Korean, Bulgarian, Rumanian, Hungarian, Mongolian, Albanian Ambassadors, Polish Chargé and acting head German Diplomatic Mission.All Communist representatives.

Press states Mao was met at USSR border by Deputy Foreign Minister Lavrentyev and others and that RoschinN. V. Roschin, Soviet Ambassador in China successively to the National Government and to the Chinese Communist regime at Peiping. arrived Moscow “simultaneously with Mao”.
At station Mao made brief radio speech. Large photo of group at station shows Mao fleshier than usual pictures.
